provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content:
1. Citation
This Act may be cited as the National Archives and Records Services Act.
[ 14 of 2007, s. 2. ]

provisions.title: Interpretation

provisions.chapter: PART II National Archives and Records Services and Places of Deposit
provisions.title: Establishment
provisions.content:
3. Establishment
There is hereby established the National Archives and Records Services of Botswana for the storage and preservation of public archives other than those which are to be kept in some other place of deposit.
[ 14 of 2007, s. 4. ]

provisions.chapter: PART II National Archives and Records Services and Places of Deposit
provisions.title: Functions of the National Archives and Records Services
provisions.content:
3A. Functions of the National Archives and Records Services
The functions of the National Archives and Records Services shall be—
( a ) to provide records and information management service to government agencies; and
( b ) to collect, preserve and access the nation's documentary heritage.
[ 14 of 2007, s. 5. ]

definitions.definition: an officer of the National Archives and Records Services authorised by the Director to do anything in accordance with the provisions of this Act
the National Archives and Records Services Advisory Council established under section 20
the Director of National Archives and Records Services appointed under section 5
any records, other than public and judicial records, which are declared as such under section 15

any records constituting the official records of any proceedings before a court, commission or board of inquiry, other judicial or quasi-judicial body or person, which—
( a ) are in the custody of—
(i) such court, commission, board, body or person
the National Archives and Records Services of Botswana established under section 3
a place of deposit declared as such under section 4
any public body, corporation, society, association, institution, organisation or any body of persons, whether incorporated or not, declared to be such by the Minister
a record created or received by a private individual or body other than an agency of the Government

any electronic records, manuscript, newspaper, picture, painting, document, register, printed material, book, map, plan, drawing, photograph, negative and positive pictures, photocopy, microfilm, cinematograph film, video tape, magnetic tape, gramophone record or other transcription of language, picture or music, recorded by any means capable of reproduction and regardless of physical form and characteristics.
[ 14 of 2007, s. 3(a). ]
